title: Turn a Table into a Story
tagline: Transform a raw data table into a 3-paragraph executive summary.
scenario: Your manager drops a table on your desk showing quarterly sales by region for the last 2 years. She says: "I need to present this to the board in 30 minutes. Give me something I can read aloud."
brief: Invent a plausible 8-quarter × 4-region sales table. Then write a 3-paragraph executive summary that tells a story: what happened, why it matters, and what the board should do about it.
deliverables: The raw data table (8 quarters × 4 regions)
A 3-paragraph executive summary: (1) the headline insight, (2) the supporting evidence, (3) the recommended action
One number you would highlight in bold if this were a slide, and why
submissionGuidance: The board does not want to read a table. They want to hear a story. Your summary must be speakable — read it aloud before submitting.

rubric.label: Data Plausibility
rubric.description: Is the invented data internally consistent and realistic?

rubric.label: Narrative Quality
rubric.description: Does the summary tell a story with a clear beginning, middle, and end?

rubric.label: Actionable Recommendation
rubric.description: Is the recommended action specific and tied to the data?

rubric.label: Key Number Selection
rubric.description: Is the highlighted number the right one to anchor the story?
